SV DISEASE: -2 SV POISON: -2
Haste: +9%
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL

Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Lore Item
Slot: HANDS
AC: 2
SV DISEASE: -2 SV POISON: -2
Haste: +9%
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: ALL
Race: ALL

Rarity: Rare

NPC Name

# Jun 07 2005 at 1:08 AM Rating: Decent

23 posts
I would sell this item for no less than 300pp
▲ Page top
RE: $$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$$
QuoteReply
# Jun 07 2005 at 10:57 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
Don't hold your breath. 9% haste is not real impressive, and 300pp is an awful lot, seeing as how FBSS sometimes gets sold for 500pp and its 21% haste.
▲ Page top
Items/lore/nodrop
QuoteReply
# Sep 16 2004 at 4:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592792408020
View User Forum
6 posts
Ok you say these be good item for newbies if higher levels could farm them for them. Well all the farming I seen highers do for lowers is eather for an alt or if they far for other people they carge out the butt look at this price 2000 pp. Thats realy reasonable and no its not cause you can only cary one of these from Runnyeye its cause high people want lots of pp for anything that might be useful to anyone under there level. Ever notice how stuff is like so dam expensive when you need it yet so cheap when you go to sell it? Its cause all the highs farm the stuff and by the time you can get to that item there farming something new. So lore and no drop items are the best thing in this game I think there should be like 90% no drop except common items for armor and weapons I mean ofcoarse. :) Food and other items should be drop and non lore. Hey what do I know I don't crunch numbers for a living or work for the the treasurey of america god knows if they were in charge our prices would drop haha yea right they would be worse so i guess we should be a little happy. Anyhow I went through runnyeye tonight I think this zone is way under used nowadays. everyone gets tied up in new expantions. I mean god at level 17-22 group in there could get some decent armor for themselfs. a rog/rng/shm and other chain class wearable 16 or was it 17 armor item is dang good at those levels but everyones twinks now so what do they know about good armor that you actualy earn.
▲ Page top
Great Lowbie Item....not.
QuoteReply
# Sep 09 2004 at 1:04 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1679271392283
Scholar
308 posts
I just love the way anything remotely farmable is 'LORE'...these would be great for high levels to sell to low levels if they weren't lore. But then, that would 'help the community' and 'make good rapport between high and low levels' which isn't what everquest is about. Thanks again, 'powergamerz'.

lvl 49
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2004 at 1:24 AM Rating: Decent
mikehill
16 posts
if your level 49 are these gloves 50% haste?
▲ Page top
RE: lvl 49
QuoteReply
# Jul 26 2004 at 11:06 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592258988162
304 posts
Nope. Haste is capped at 9%. That level thing is for the Spell haste, not haste on items, which is always set at a fixed %, regardless of level. ;)

Listen, HELOOO, 45s can defenetly not farm lguk. There are so many ads, and if u go to frenzied, 6 will jump u along with the frenzied, they all average bout lvl 40, and getting there is a pain. # Mar 16 2003 at 6:27 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592256924541
11 posts
ok so i bought these today for 600pp from this desperate guy. Keep in mind that at the bazaar i looked them up and they were around 1.5k. So just for giggles i do a PC in the nexus. And what do you know someone tries to tell me that it is only 2% haste and is worth no more than 100pp. LMAO!! So i just ignored him and whadya know a few min later he wants to buy them from me for 100pp!!! Is that a scam or what?!!! 2% haste!! He could have made his lie better by saying 5% haste!! LOL

27th monk of zeb

ok i got a question, according to info here if im lvl 50 this will be like what 49% haste????? omfg is this true? im farming this beatch if it is Thanks Jumangi Fuzzynutzz

▲ Page top
RE: Haste?>?>?
QuoteReply
# Mar 03 2003 at 4:49 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
What?

No. The haste info you're reading is for the spell "Haste". Worn Haste effect items are fixed values. This, for example, is 9%.

▲ Page top
Gloves
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2003 at 8:01 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591781710537
17 posts
Well I was there in Runnyeye last night. I go there every so often to see what named is up and to try to find some stuff to sell. Ended up finding the Moldmaster on track. Am a 51 druid so as you can imagine he died a horrible death lol. It is true though i had about 4 adds during the battle, Luckily I was able to get upheaval off twice and killed them all. Looted the body and what to my wondering eye's should appear? The gloves WOOT. They found a home on my level 13 Darkelf Warrior. Killing him takes standard druid dungeon tactics. Root, dot, nuke, root, nuke repeat. Only takes about one drifting death, two starfires and maybe 3 root's to kill him. Just make sure you have a gate spell handy, Just in case you get to many adds and need to pop out. Also even though everything is green to you if you have killed several of the sporalis they will jump you if they see you. Keep camo up while you track and time your assault to limit the amount of wonderers around. And there will be alot of wanderers.

Keggan
There is no reason for a druid to be broke.
▲ Page top
Haste
QuoteReply
# Jan 15 2003 at 8:46 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591765706802
Scholar
35 posts
The haste on this item is 9% according to http://eq.jeuxonline.info/guides/objets/haste.php

▲ Page top
This guy does suc!<
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2003 at 2:03 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593374958763
10 posts
Just walked my 51 monk into Runnyeye to grab this guy's shield and scout around a bit for my new Enchanter's prospective next hunting grounds. Figured I'm about twice the level of anything in this place, so it should be a walk through Care Bear park. Not quite.

Got to this guy and jumped on him. I was at half life (from killing the halfling guards on the way) but he's only 25. Not a problem, right? I solo level 40 mobs at half life for xp after all.

Nope. Burnt myself down quick on his damage shield (I was dealing more damage to myself than he could hit me for [had the sword btw]). He got me with one dot, but I couldn't really measure how much damage it did since I was going down so fast from the DS.

Backed off, feigned, played a few games of gems, and mended to full. Took him on again (despawned and had shield now, which made me happy). Pummelled him down pretty quick but was still at half life when he turned and started running. And this guy runs FAST. Couldn't catch him. He got up onto the second floor, dodged in out and of hallways really quickly, and eventually lost me. So I pulled an old tactic and sat down, figuring he'd come back and fight then. He did, but not until after he cast a few heals and brought himself up to half as well.

When he charged back, I took him out, but I honestly think it would have just been a stalemate if he'd ran again. I don't know how I could have possibly ever caught him. Nukes hit twice the second time for 97 and he slowed me a TON. That might be part of the problem everyone else says is with the regen. I didn't notice him healing alot other than with heal spells, but when you get slowed on top of their regen, I can see how it might have been difficult to mass up the damage to finish him off.

~Personally, I think undercons in the game are really DUMB. If a level twenty-six character can kill this guy, he deserves alot better than blue xp.

What's the haste on the gloves, btw?
▲ Page top
Level 20? Ya RIGHT
QuoteReply
# Nov 04 2002 at 5:45 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593966240332
5 posts
Someone's gotta be kidding me. To get these gloves to drop you'd need a FULL group of level 20's. I took my 30 druid to Runnyeye to try and get some of these, and druids, great soloers though they are will notice a real problem with this guy. His resists are really high and he's a caster with a solid Root and Slashed me (with approx. 400 AC) for 60 consistently.
I think the best way to get these things is to get a good tank and then nuke the crap out of him AFTER he's been snared (He moves fast). I'm gonna go try for them again, with guildie help this time.

No time for fancy words, or a fancy saying. I'm logging on EQ now to do some major Slaying!

Braldiz Wildfury
30 Druid <Battle Forged>
Solusek Ro
▲ Page top
RE: Level 20? Ya RIGHT
QuoteReply
# Dec 20 2002 at 1:22 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770377803
73 posts
Silly druid. I know i cant solo in a dungeon very easily unless everything is green. I almost go killed at 53 in Runneye with my wizard. No AC/HPs plus damage limited to how much mana you have makes it tough.
▲ Page top
gloves still drop
QuoteReply
# Oct 21 2002 at 9:40 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251929342
View User Forum
12 posts
in the past week, I've killed Moldy five times....he's dropped the sword once, the funky shield four times (three times let it rot) and last time......the gloves! My monk will like these till he can get a FBSS. Someone above posted they are 12%haste, a guildie says 9%. Can anyone larify this for sure? Had someone offer me 1500pp when they dropped and I said no......this is a great twinky item IMHO.
▲ Page top
Talk about karma...
QuoteReply
# Sep 19 2002 at 5:00 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591763827059
4 posts
I had spent many a night on the named critters in Runnyeye. Now I only go back to have a little fun (and maybe try to find an Alloy Medallion).
As uncommon as old Moldy is, and as rare as he carries these gloves, I never saw them drop during my XP hunts.
Cut to 20 levels later, and a guildy distresses over going LD in Runnyeye and coming back DEAD (I guess the NPC AI doesn't know how to keep a 52nd Lvl Bard alive against critters 15-20 Lvls lower :-( ). I tried to find a cleric willing to travel to rezz her, but the three zones I hit had no takers. I head to Runnyeye to offer her company, to find her partnered with a 56th Lvl warrior, and just slaughtering anything that moved. Since they had things well in hand I went to check Moldy's spawn points, and LO! there he was. And wouldn't you know he had the gloves on him!?
I offered the gloves to my guildmate, since they would have been hers no doubt before the unfortunate LD incident, but she said she'd stick with her Singing steel gauntlets. Looks like my young Paladin's about to get a bit faster :-)

Valkir
▲ Page top
They still drop
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2002 at 12:02 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781681498
1 post
Just messing around in Runnyeye and Moldy popped, he dropped the gloves...yeah lucky me!

So they are still dropping just very rare as they have always been
